title: refactor(mcp): remove unused install script, getApiKey, and dotenv dep
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Delete dead install-claude-mcp.js (76 lines); it was never in package.json `files`, so the documented `npx @pollinations/mcp install-claude-mcp` command never worked
- Fix README "Claude Desktop Integration" to point straight at the existing manual claude_desktop_config.json snippet (drops the broken npx block)
- Remove getApiKey() from authUtils.js and its unused import in authService.js (no callers in the package)
- Drop unused dotenv dependency from package.json + package-lock.json (not imported anywhere in the package)

Risks: none — pure dead-code removal, no callers, no behavior change. Stale "uses dotenv" line in GITHUB_AUTH.md left untouched (out of scope; doc-only).

Deferred (out of scope / premise changed):
- Optional GITHUB_AUTH.md doc cleanup (stale 'uses dotenv to load environment variables' lin — Explicitly marked optional and 'not part of the dead-code removal' in the Finding 3 proposal, and GITHUB_AUTH.md is not 

_~108 net lines removed · biome ✓ · No runnable unit tests for changed code. packages/mcp test script is `node test-mcp-client ✓_

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the MCP
gist.blurb: Swept away some unused code and dependencies in the MCP package to keep the hive light and breezy.
gist.summary: Removed the unused install-claude-mcp.js script, getApiKey function, and dotenv dependency from the @pollinations/mcp package.
gist.impact: The MCP package is now leaner with ~108 lines of dead code removed, and the README correctly points to manual Claude Desktop configuration instead of a broken npx command.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot sweeping or cleaning up dead leaves/code blocks in a cozy digital garden, while the Nomnom creature eats the dead code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny scarf, sweeping up pixelated dead leaves and old code blocks with a small broom. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of dusty documents. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
